content_en:
side. These people include *Abdullaah Ibn as-Sawdaa’, Kinaanah Ibn Bishr and others,

The caliph wanted to make sure himself that the conclusion his three delegates reached was true and to clear himself before his Lord and his subjects from any oppression that could have been inflicted on any of the people. Therefore, he issued an official announcement to be delivered to all provinces in which he called upon all those who were wronged or had a complaint to come to Makkah during pilgrimage time to present their complaints before the caliph so that he would retaliate on their behalf against the oppressor and restore to them their rights. In 34 AH, people came to Makkah for the pilgrimage, finished their rituals and then returned to their provinces. None of them stayed to file a complaint.

However, it was not a matter of individual complaints or personal incidents of oppression which could be settled, so as to free souls from grudges, let hearts rest. so that the Muslim public would be satisfied by the administration of the Ummah affairs. The situation was much more dangerous than that, because a group of those who were hostile to ~Uthmaan from Kufah, Basrah and Egypt reached Madeenah for a secretlycontrived conspiracy. They explained their plan saying: "We want to mention to the caliph certain ideas which we indoctrinate people to believe. We will then return to the people and claim that he admitted them, and did not give them up or repent. Next we will dress as pilgrims, besiege him and depose him. If he refuses, we will kill him." That was the exact scenario that took place.

The caliph managed to find out about the intentions of those rebels through two persons who were close to them and briefed the caliph on their thoughts. The Companions of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, advised * Uthmaan to

kill them in order to rid the Ummah of their danger and nip the sedition in the bud on the ground of the saying of the Prophet: "Whoever calls for himself or for someone else while people are under the leadership of one Imam, he is cursed by Allah, so kill him." Nevertheless, ~Uthmaan, as was his habit, treated them with leniency and did not listen the advice of the Companions who thought they ought to be handled with more firmness. Instead, he said, “No, we will forgive them and inform them of our efforts. We will never punish them unless they commit a punishable crime or declare disbelief."

As regards the issues and questions they implanted in the hearts of people and thought the caliph would be unable to answer and so be forced to admit that he was wrong, ~ Uthmaan discussed them and persuaded them that his conduct was valid. They thus accepted the majority of his views and kept silent as to few of them. These issues are as follows:

They said, “He did not shorten the prayer, when traveling, though it used to be shortened during the lifetimes of the Prophet and his two Companions (Abu Bakr and ‘Umar)." *Uthmaan answered, "I arrived at a city where my family resides so I stayed and completed the prayer." They were content with his answer.

They argued, "He nominated a certain land for for the camels of Sadagah to graze on though the Prophet and his two Companions did not do so." He answered that (he did so) so that the camels of people should not mingle with them and thus compete with them in grazing. He also added that he had only two camels for the journey of Hajj though when he was first appointed as caliph he was the greatest owner of camels and sheep amongst the Arabs. They were satisfied with this answer.

They said that he collected the Quran in one Mushaf (i.e. book) and ordered that all other Mushafs be burnt. He answered, "Truly the Quran is one (book), revealed by the One (Allah), and in doing, so I was a follower and not a heretic." They were content with his answer.

They said that he permitted Al-Hakam Ibn Abi Al-* Aas — his uncle — to return to Makkah whom the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, exiled to Al-Taa'if, because he was one of the most hostile pagans to the Prophet in Makkah before migration. He remained in exile during the rule of AbuBakr. They saw no grounds for * Uthmaan to permit him to return (to Makkah). He answered: "I interceded with Allah's Messenger for his sake and he accepted my intercession. Yet, the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him, had died before Al-Hakam's return, and Abu Bakr and ‘Umar refused to accept a report transmitted by only one person. So when I came to office, I allow him to come back." They were satisfied with the answer, too.

They said that he appointed young people particularly from his own family. He answered that the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, appointed Zayd Ibn Haarithah and after him appointed Zayd's son, Usamah, as commander of an army which included many of the great Companions. Some people argued with him about that at that time. They also accepted this answer,

They said that he conferred on * Abdullaah Ibn Abi Sarh —his foster-brother — one fifth of the fifth to be allocated to the public treasury. He answered that both Abu Bakr and ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with them, behaved in the same way. But he got the money back from Ibn Abi Sarh when he knew that the soldiers disliked it. The people were pleased with this behavior.

They said: "The caliph loves his family and confers on them plentiful wealth." He answered: "I love them but I am not biased towards them thus doing injustice (to anybody) nor do I give them what is not rightfully theirs. I only give them from my own property and I do not deem it lawful for myself or any other person to take any of the properties of Muslims.” The caliph added: "I used to spend generously during the lifetimes of the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, Abu Bakr and ‘Umar, may Allah be pleased with them, while I was still stingy at that time, then how do you blame me when I grew old and conferred on my family what I owned?"

Finally, they said, "The caliph permitted the great Companions to go to different provinces such as Iraq, Syria and Egypt where they bought and possessed properties though his predecessor, ‘Umar, prevented them from leaving the capital lest people should be tempted to follow their example and thus be occupied with trade and the amassing of wealth." This stance is in conformity with the lenient and kind character of the caliph in dealing with people. ~Uthmaan answered them saying: "These lands have been shared by their brothers, the helpers and immigrants, when they were conquered. So whoever stays anywhere in those lands would be an example for its people to follow." They also kept silent without agreeing with him concerning this issue.

Those remarks and oppositions to * Uthmaan's policy and conduct in administering the affairs of the Ummah particularly with regard to appointing his relatives in public offices and conferring many gifts on them undoubtedly constituted the main reasons behind people's exasperation with ~Uthmaan's authority. That was made excellent use of by the rebels to incite people and arouse their feelings against him.

Although that discussion officially ended with these people's silence, the caliph realized that their attitudes would have some repercussions and that he should wisely handle the matter before the situation deteriorated. He thus sent to his governors in the different regions asking them to meet him during the pilgrimage time to discuss the issue.

The Caliph's Meeting with the Governors of Provinces:

According to At-Tabari and others, * Uthmaan sent to the governors of the provinces: ~Abdullaah Ibn * Amir, Mu’ awiyyah Ibn Abi Sufyyaan, and * Abdullaah Ibn Sa*d Ibn Abi Sarh. On their arrival, ~ Uthmaan asked Sa*eed Ibn Al-* As and ‘Amr Ibn AI--As_ to participate in the consultation. *Uthmaan, then, wondered: “What are the reason behind all these complaints? I am afraid lest these complaints should turn out to be founded, which, in turn, would disgrace me personally.” They said: “Haven't you sent them your messengers? Haven't we told you the affairs of the people? Haven't they come back and_ no one has spoken to them? By Allah, they are neither truthful nor loyal? We do not know the source of these complaints? You can judge anyone according to them? They are merely false statements that no one can rely on.” He said: “What is your opinion?” Sa* eed Ibn Al-* As said, “This was all secretly fabricated. Then, people, who do not know the reality, are to spread it amongst people.” ~Uthmaan said: “What is the solution then?” He replied: “Bring the persons who spread it and kill them.” *Abdullaah Ibn Sa*d said: “Take from people what they owe you after you give them their rights for this is better than leaving them.” Mu’ aawiyyah said: “You have appointed me (as a governor) on some people from whom you can only expect what is good.” *Uthmaan asked: “What do you say, *Amr?” * Amr answered: “It seems to me that you have dealt with them leniently and so encouraged them to do so. You should follow in the footsteps of your two

companions by behaving harshly when it is necessary and behaving leniently in proper times. Wicked people should be handled harshly, while leniency is the recompense of good people. You, however, deal with both parties alike, i.e. with leniency.”

Thus, everyone who was consulted saw that ~ Uthmaan had to deal strictly with those people who instigated sedition. Owing to his tender nature, ~Uthmaan did not listen to their opinions. Instead, he commanded them to give those people their rights, to excuse them, and to forcefully combat them if Allah's rights were violated.

Now the question is: Did those governors abide by *Uthmaan's commands? The answer is no. They neither prevented people from revolting against the Caliph, nor did they give people their rights by explaining to them the bad consequences of the revolt. Further, they did not protect Allah's rights for, by leaving those people on the loose, they would disobey the Caliph and spread disorder in the provinces.

Each governor returned back to his province. Mu aawiyyah, who realized the difficult situation * Uthmaan was in, offered the latter to come join him in Syria. ~ Uthmaan, however, refused to leave Madeenah, where the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, was buried. Mu’ aawiyyah, alternatively, offered ~Uthmaan to send some Syrian soldiers to defend him against the expected danger. Yet, ~Uthmaan rejected the idea fearing that foreign soldiers may annoy people of Madeenah.
content_id:
pihak. Orang-orang ini termasuk *Abdullaah Ibn as-Sawdaa', Kinaanah Ibn Bishr dan lainnya,

Khalifah ingin memastikan sendiri bahwa kesimpulan yang dicapai oleh ketiga delegasinya adalah benar dan untuk membersihkan dirinya di hadapan Tuhannya dan rakyatnya dari kezaliman apa pun yang mungkin telah menimpa seseorang. Oleh karena itu, ia mengeluarkan pengumuman resmi yang disampaikan ke seluruh provinsi, di mana ia mengundang semua orang yang dizalimi atau memiliki keluhan untuk datang ke Makkah pada musim haji untuk menyampaikan keluhan mereka di hadapan khalifah, sehingga ia dapat membalas atas nama mereka terhadap pihak yang zalim dan memulihkan hak-hak mereka. Pada tahun 34 H, orang-orang datang ke Makkah untuk menunaikan ibadah haji, menyelesaikan ritualnya, kemudian kembali ke provinsi mereka. Tidak seorang pun yang tinggal untuk mengajukan keluhan.

Namun, ini bukan persoalan keluhan individual atau insiden kezaliman pribadi yang dapat diselesaikan, sehingga jiwa terbebas dari dendam dan hati menjadi tenteram, agar masyarakat Muslim merasa puas dengan pengelolaan urusan ummah. Situasinya jauh lebih berbahaya dari itu, karena sekelompok orang yang memusuhi 'Uthmaan dari Kufah, Basrah dan Mesir tiba di Madeenah untuk menjalankan konspirasi yang dirancang secara rahasia. Mereka menjelaskan rencana mereka dengan berkata: "Kami ingin menyebutkan kepada khalifah gagasan-gagasan tertentu yang kami doktrinkan kepada orang-orang agar dipercaya. Kemudian kami akan kembali kepada orang-orang dan mengklaim bahwa ia mengakui gagasan tersebut, dan tidak melepaskannya atau bertaubat. Selanjutnya kami akan berpakaian seperti jamaah haji, mengepungnya dan menggulingkannya. Jika ia menolak, kami akan membunuhnya." Itulah skenario yang terjadi.

Khalifah berhasil mengetahui niat para pemberontak tersebut melalui dua orang yang dekat dengan mereka dan memberikan informasi kepada khalifah tentang pikiran mereka. Para Sahabat Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, menyarankan 'Uthmaan untuk

membunuh mereka demi membebaskan ummah dari bahaya mereka dan menghentikan fitnah sejak awal berdasarkan sabda Nabi: "Barangsiapa yang menyeru untuk dirinya sendiri atau untuk orang lain sementara orang-orang berada di bawah kepemimpinan seorang Imam, maka ia dilaknat oleh Allah, maka bunuhlah ia." Namun demikian, 'Uthmaan, sesuai dengan kebiasaannya, memperlakukan mereka dengan kelembutan dan tidak mendengarkan nasihat para Sahabat yang berpendapat bahwa mereka harus ditangani dengan lebih tegas. Sebaliknya, ia berkata, "Tidak, kami akan memaafkan mereka dan memberitahu mereka tentang upaya kami. Kami tidak akan pernah menghukum mereka kecuali mereka melakukan kejahatan yang dapat dihukum atau menyatakan kekufuran."

Adapun mengenai permasalahan dan pertanyaan yang mereka tanamkan di hati masyarakat dan mereka kira khalifah tidak akan mampu menjawabnya sehingga terpaksa mengakui kesalahannya, 'Uthmaan mendiskusikannya dan meyakinkan mereka bahwa tindakannya adalah valid. Mereka pun menerima sebagian besar pandangannya dan diam terhadap sebagian kecilnya. Permasalahan-permasalahan tersebut adalah sebagai berikut:

Mereka berkata, "Ia tidak mengqashar shalat ketika bepergian, padahal dahulu shalat diringkas pada masa Nabi dan kedua Sahabatnya (Abu Bakr dan 'Umar)." 'Uthmaan menjawab, "Aku tiba di sebuah kota di mana keluargaku tinggal sehingga aku menetap dan menyempurnakan shalat." Mereka puas dengan jawabannya.

Mereka berargumen, "Ia menetapkan sebidang tanah untuk unta-unta shadaqah merumput, padahal Nabi dan kedua Sahabatnya tidak melakukan hal tersebut." Ia menjawab bahwa (ia melakukannya) agar unta-unta milik masyarakat tidak bercampur dengan unta-unta tersebut sehingga bersaing dalam merumput. Ia juga menambahkan bahwa ia hanya memiliki dua ekor unta untuk perjalanan haji, padahal ketika pertama kali diangkat sebagai khalifah ia adalah pemilik unta dan kambing terbesar di antara orang-orang Arab. Mereka puas dengan jawaban ini.

Mereka berkata bahwa ia mengumpulkan al-Quran dalam satu Mushaf (yaitu kitab) dan memerintahkan agar semua Mushaf lainnya dibakar. Ia menjawab, "Sungguh al-Quran itu satu (kitab), diturunkan oleh Yang Maha Esa (Allah), dan dalam melakukan hal itu, aku adalah pengikut dan bukan pembuat bid'ah." Mereka puas dengan jawabannya.

Mereka berkata bahwa ia mengizinkan Al-Hakam Ibn Abi Al-'Aas — pamannya — untuk kembali ke Makkah, yang diasingkan oleh Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, ke Al-Taa'if, karena ia adalah salah satu orang musyrik yang paling memusuhi Nabi di Makkah sebelum hijrah. Ia tetap dalam pengasingan semasa pemerintahan Abu Bakr. Mereka tidak melihat alasan bagi 'Uthmaan untuk mengizinkannya kembali (ke Makkah). Ia menjawab: "Aku pernah memohon syafa'at kepada Rasulullah untuknya dan beliau menerima syafa'atku. Namun Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, telah wafat sebelum Al-Hakam kembali, dan Abu Bakr serta 'Umar menolak menerima laporan yang hanya disampaikan oleh satu orang. Maka ketika aku menjabat, aku mengizinkannya untuk kembali." Mereka juga puas dengan jawabannya.

Mereka berkata bahwa ia mengangkat orang-orang muda khususnya dari keluarganya sendiri. Ia menjawab bahwa Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, mengangkat Zayd Ibn Haarithah dan setelahnya mengangkat putra Zayd, Usamah, sebagai panglima pasukan yang mencakup banyak Sahabat terkemuka. Beberapa orang berdebat dengannya mengenai hal itu pada saat itu. Mereka juga menerima jawaban ini.

Mereka berkata bahwa ia memberikan kepada *Abdullaah Ibn Abi Sarh — saudara susunya — seperlima dari seperlima yang dialokasikan untuk kas negara. Ia menjawab bahwa baik Abu Bakr maupun 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhuma, berperilaku dengan cara yang sama. Namun ia mengambil kembali uang tersebut dari Ibn Abi Sarh ketika mengetahui bahwa para prajurit tidak menyukainya. Orang-orang puas dengan sikap ini.

Mereka berkata: "Khalifah mencintai keluarganya dan memberikan kekayaan yang melimpah kepada mereka." Ia menjawab: "Aku mencintai mereka tetapi aku tidak berpihak kepada mereka sehingga berbuat zalim (kepada siapa pun) dan aku tidak memberikan mereka apa yang bukan hak mereka. Aku hanya memberi mereka dari hartaku sendiri dan aku tidak menghalalkan bagi diriku atau orang lain untuk mengambil harta kaum Muslimin." Khalifah menambahkan: "Aku dahulu biasa berinfaq dengan dermawan pada masa Rasulullah, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, Abu Bakr dan 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhuma, sementara aku masih kikir pada masa itu, lalu bagaimana kalian mencercaku ketika aku telah tua dan memberikan kepada keluargaku apa yang aku miliki?"

Akhirnya, mereka berkata, "Khalifah mengizinkan para Sahabat terkemuka pergi ke berbagai provinsi seperti Iraq, Syam dan Mesir di mana mereka membeli dan memiliki harta, padahal pendahulunya, 'Umar, melarang mereka meninggalkan ibu kota agar masyarakat tidak tergoda untuk mengikuti contoh mereka dan dengan demikian tersibukkan dengan perdagangan dan penimbunan kekayaan." Sikap ini sesuai dengan karakter khalifah yang lemah lembut dan baik hati dalam bergaul dengan masyarakat. 'Uthmaan menjawab mereka dengan berkata: "Tanah-tanah ini telah dibagi di antara saudara-saudara mereka, para anshar dan muhajirin, ketika tanah-tanah itu ditaklukkan. Maka barangsiapa yang menetap di mana saja di tanah-tanah itu akan menjadi teladan bagi penduduknya untuk diikuti." Mereka juga diam tanpa menyetujuinya mengenai masalah ini.

Kecaman-kecaman dan pertentangan terhadap kebijakan dan perilaku 'Uthmaan dalam mengelola urusan ummah, khususnya terkait pengangkatan kerabatnya dalam jabatan publik dan pemberian banyak hadiah kepada mereka, tanpa diragukan lagi merupakan alasan utama di balik kejenuhan masyarakat terhadap kekuasaan 'Uthmaan. Hal ini dimanfaatkan sebaik-baiknya oleh para pemberontak untuk menghasut masyarakat dan membangkitkan perasaan mereka melawannya.

Meskipun diskusi tersebut secara resmi berakhir dengan keheningan orang-orang ini, khalifah menyadari bahwa sikap mereka akan menimbulkan dampak dan bahwa ia harus menangani masalah ini dengan bijaksana sebelum situasi memburuk. Ia pun mengirim surat kepada para gubernurnya di berbagai wilayah meminta mereka untuk bertemu dengannya pada musim haji guna membahas masalah tersebut.

Pertemuan Khalifah dengan Para Gubernur Provinsi:

Menurut At-Tabari dan lainnya, 'Uthmaan mengirim surat kepada para gubernur provinsi: 'Abdullaah Ibn 'Amir, Mu'awiyyah Ibn Abi Sufyyaan, dan 'Abdullaah Ibn Sa'd Ibn Abi Sarh. Ketika mereka tiba, 'Uthmaan meminta Sa'eed Ibn Al-'As dan 'Amr Ibn Al-'As untuk ikut serta dalam musyawarah. 'Uthmaan kemudian bertanya-tanya: "Apa alasan di balik semua keluhan ini? Aku khawatir jangan-jangan keluhan-keluhan ini ternyata beralasan, yang pada gilirannya akan mempermalukan aku secara pribadi." Mereka berkata: "Bukankah engkau telah mengirimkan utusan kepada mereka? Bukankah kami telah memberitahumu urusan masyarakat? Bukankah mereka telah kembali dan tidak ada seorang pun yang berbicara kepada mereka? Demi Allah, mereka tidak jujur maupun setia. Kami tidak tahu dari mana asal keluhan-keluhan ini. Engkau dapat menghakimi siapa pun berdasarkan keluhan-keluhan itu. Itu hanyalah pernyataan-pernyataan palsu yang tidak dapat diandalkan oleh siapa pun." Ia berkata: "Apa pendapatmu?" Sa'eed Ibn Al-'As berkata, "Semua ini dirancang secara rahasia. Kemudian orang-orang yang tidak mengetahui kenyataannya menyebarkannya di antara masyarakat." 'Uthmaan berkata: "Lalu apa solusinya?" Ia menjawab: "Hadirkan orang-orang yang menyebarkannya dan bunuh mereka." 'Abdullaah Ibn Sa'd berkata: "Ambillah dari masyarakat apa yang mereka hutangkan kepadamu setelah engkau memberikan hak-hak mereka, karena ini lebih baik daripada membiarkan mereka." Mu'aawiyyah berkata: "Engkau telah mengangkatku (sebagai gubernur) atas sejumlah orang dari mereka yang hanya dapat kau harapkan kebaikannya." 'Uthmaan bertanya: "Apa yang kamu katakan, 'Amr?" 'Amr menjawab: "Tampaknya engkau telah memperlakukan mereka dengan lemah lembut sehingga mendorong mereka untuk berbuat demikian. Engkau seharusnya mengikuti jejak kedua

para sahabat dengan bersikap keras ketika diperlukan dan bersikap lembut pada saat yang tepat. Orang-orang jahat harus ditangani dengan keras, sementara kelembutan adalah balasan bagi orang-orang baik. Namun engkau memperlakukan kedua pihak sama, yakni dengan kelembutan."

Dengan demikian, setiap orang yang dimintai pendapat berpandangan bahwa 'Utsman harus bersikap tegas terhadap orang-orang yang memicu fitnah. Karena sifatnya yang lembut, 'Utsman tidak mendengarkan pendapat mereka. Sebaliknya, beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk memberikan hak-hak kepada orang-orang tersebut, memaafkan mereka, dan memerangi mereka dengan sungguh-sungguh jika hak-hak Allah dilanggar.

Kini pertanyaannya adalah: Apakah para gubernur tersebut mematuhi perintah 'Utsman? Jawabannya tidak. Mereka tidak mencegah orang-orang dari pemberontakan terhadap Khalifah, tidak pula memberikan hak-hak rakyat dengan menjelaskan kepada mereka akibat buruk dari pemberontakan itu. Lebih jauh, mereka tidak menjaga hak-hak Allah, karena dengan membiarkan orang-orang tersebut bebas, mereka akan mendurhakai Khalifah dan menyebarkan kerusuhan di berbagai wilayah.

Setiap gubernur kembali ke wilayahnya masing-masing. Mu'awiyyah, yang menyadari situasi sulit yang dihadapi 'Utsman, menawarkan kepada beliau untuk bergabung dengannya di Syam. Namun 'Utsman menolak meninggalkan Madinah, tempat Nabi, semoga shalawat dan salam terlimpah kepada beliau, dimakamkan. Sebagai alternatif, Mu'awiyyah menawarkan untuk mengirimkan sejumlah pasukan Syam guna melindungi 'Utsman dari bahaya yang diperkirakan akan datang. Namun 'Utsman menolak gagasan tersebut karena khawatir pasukan asing itu akan mengganggu penduduk Madinah.
content_ar: side. These people include *Abdullaah Ibn as-Sawdaa’, Kinaanah Ibn Bishr and others, The caliph wanted to make sure himself that the conclusion his three delegates reached was true and to clear himself قبل his Lord and his subjects from any oppression that could have been inflicted on any of the people. لذلك, he issued an official announcement to be delivered to all provinces in which he called upon all those who were wronged or had a complaint to come to مكة المكرمة خلال pilgrimage time to present their complaints قبل the caliph لذا that he would retaliate on their behalf against the oppressor and restore to them their الحقوق. In 34 AH, people came to مكة المكرمة for the pilgrimage, finished their rituals and then returned to their provinces. None of them stayed to file a complaint. غير أن, it was not a matter of individual complaints or personal incidents of oppression which could be settled, لذا as to free النفوس from grudges, let القلوب rest. لذا that the مسلم public would be satisfied by the administration of the الأمة affairs. The situation was much more dangerous than that, لأن a group of those who were hostile to ~Uthmaan from Kufah, Basrah and Egypt reached Madeenah for a secretlycontrived conspiracy. They explained their plan saying: "We want to mention to the caliph certain ideas which we indoctrinate people to believe. We will then return to the people and claim that he admitted them, and did not give them up or repent. Next we will dress as pilgrims, besiege him and depose him. إذا he refuses, we will kill him." That was the exact scenario that took place. The caliph managed to find out about the intentions of those rebels through two persons who were close to them and briefed the caliph on their thoughts. The الصحابة of النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, advised * Uthmaan to kill them من أجل rid the الأمة of their danger and nip the sedition in the bud on the ground of the saying of النبي ﷺ: "Whoever calls for himself or for someone else بينما people are under the leadership of one الإمام, he is cursed by الله, لذا kill him." على الرغم من ذلك, ~Uthmaan, as was his habit, treated them with leniency and did not listen the advice of the الصحابة who thought they ought to be handled with more firmness. Instead, قال, “No, we will forgive them and inform them of our efforts. We will never punish them إلا إذا they commit a punishable crime or declare disbelief." As regards the issues and questions they implanted in the القلوب of people and thought the caliph would be unable to answer and لذا be forced to admit that he was الخطأ, ~ Uthmaan discussed them and persuaded them that his السلوك was valid. They وهكذا accepted the majority of his views and kept silent as to few of them. These issues are as follows: قالوا, “He did not shorten the الصلاة, عندما traveling, though it used to be shortened خلال the lifetimes of النبي ﷺ and his two الصحابة (أبو بكر and ‘Um
